title: The Columbus Timestamp Contradiction
description: Cross-referencing the red fire dispatch timestamp against the Erie rail schedule in the depot logbook reveals that no passenger train ran from Columbus to the Erie corridor today — and a man on horseback would require at least eighteen hours to cover the distance. Greer filed the dispatch claiming a Columbus origin at 9:47 PM, but if he is close enough to Sandusky to act on the bounty tonight, he was already north of Columbus when he filed. He used a relay operator to send the message under a false origin station, placing himself legally in Columbus while physically near the target.
